drugs.efficacy_snippet: CLINICAL TRIALS Duodenal Ulcer Cimetidine tablets have been shown to be effective in the treatment of active duodenal ulcer and, at reduced dosage, in maintenance therapy following healing of active ulcers. Active Duodenal Ulcer Cimetidine tablets accelerate the rate of duodenal ulcer healing. Heali

key_results: The healing rate with 800 mg at bedtime was significantly superior to 400 mg at bedtime (66%) and not significantly different from 1600 mg at bedtime (81%).
In the U.S. dose-ranging trial, over 80% of patients receiving 800 mg of cimetidine tablets at bedtime experienced nocturnal pain relief after one day.

In numerous placebo-controlled studies conducted worldwide, the percent of patients with observed ulcers at the end of 1 year’s therapy with 400 mg of cimetidine tablets at bedtime was significantly lower (10% to 45%) than in patients receiving placebo (44% to 70%).

key_results: 14.1 Healing of Erosive Esophagitis in Adults Two multicenter, double-blind, active-controlled, randomized, eight week studies were conducted in patients with endoscopically confirmed EE.
Patients were randomized to one of the following three treatment groups: Dexlansoprazole 60 mg once daily, dexlansoprazole 90 mg once daily or lansoprazole 30 mg once daily.
Race was distributed as follows: 87% Caucasian, 5% Black and 8% Other.
Based on the Los Angeles Classification, 71% of patients had mild EE (Grades A and B) and 29% of patients had moderate to severe EE (Grades C and D) before treatment.

key_results: 14.1 Healing of Erosive Esophagitis in Adults Two multicenter, double-blind, active-controlled, randomized, eight week studies were conducted in patients with endoscopically confirmed EE.
Patients were randomized to one of the following three treatment groups: dexlansoprazole 60 mg once daily, dexlansoprazole 90 mg once daily or lansoprazole 30 mg once daily.
Race was distributed as follows: 87% Caucasian, 5% Black and 8% Other.

drugs.efficacy_snippet: 14.1 Erosive Esophagitis (EE) Associated with Gastroesophageal Reflux Disease (GERD) Adult Patients A US multicenter, double-blind, placebo-controlled study of PROTONIX 10 mg, 20 mg, or 40 mg once daily was conducted in 603 patients with reflux symptoms and endoscopically diagnosed EE of grade 2 or

key_results: A significantly greater proportion of patients taking PROTONIX 40 mg experienced complete relief of daytime and nighttime heartburn and the absence of regurgitation, starting from the first day of treatment, compared with placebo.
For the 40 mg treatment group, significantly greater healing rates compared to nizatidine were achieved regardless of the H. pylori status.

drugs.efficacy_snippet: 14.1 Gastroesophageal Reflux Disease (GERD) Associated with a History of Erosive Esophagitis (EE) A multicenter, double-blind, two-period placebo-controlled study was conducted to assess the ability of PROTONIX ® I.V. to maintain gastric acid suppression in patients switched from pantoprazole sodium

key_results: This study demonstrated that, after 10 days of repeated oral administration followed by 7 days of intravenous administration, the oral and intravenous dosage forms of pantoprazole 40 mg are similar in their ability to suppress MAO and BAO in patients with GERD and a history of EE (see Table 5 ).
However, at 48 hours after their last oral dose, patients treated with PROTONIX I.V. had a significantly lower mean basal acid output (see Table 5 ) than those treated with placebo.
